publications.editedComment: A tiny adapter with excellent labels! It reads the active Builder configuration and sheet, resolves the correct art purpose and image role, then passes title, description, model title, prompt, and context into `art-builder`. Adventure and character configs become character art; scenario, reward, and dream configs receive their matching purposes; everything else stays builder art. Updates are reduced to art prompt, image path, and ArtImage id before `store.updateArt`. Refine prompt delegates to `callArtSuggest`, and Use this asset requires nonblank prompt text before `finishCard`. A selected image gets a preview with its resolved role label. No duplicate generator lives here.

Keep the cargo light. This Component does not build art controls again; it configures `art-builder` from the active Builder sheet. Explicit art configuration wins, then project-level fields, then model-type fallbacks determine purpose, role, title, and description. The child’s update payload is narrowed into `store.updateArt`. Refine prompt calls the Builder store’s suggestion action. Use this asset calls `finishCard` and stays disabled until the sheet’s art prompt contains text. When an image path exists, the preview shows the model title and the resolved asset role. A clean transfer station: context in, three art fields out, next card only on command.

One job, clearly labeled: choose a remaining card! The header reads the active Builder title and splash tagline. Random card delegates directly to `store.randomCard()`. When `visibleCards` contains entries, the Component lays them out from one column through five responsive columns and hands each record to `builder-card`. When none remain, it shows Deck complete and explains that required fields are filled. There is no filtering, answer mutation, completion calculation, or navigation logic here; the Builder store supplies the already-visible deck and each child card handles selection. A very small gallery shell, which is exactly why it is easy to trust.

The grid is a dispatch board, not the dispatcher. It reads `activeConfig.title`, the splash tagline, and `visibleCards` from the Builder store. Each visible record becomes a `builder-card`; the layout expands from one to five columns according to viewport width. Random card asks the store to choose. Once the store returns no visible cards, the entire grid is replaced by a Deck complete state. This Component does not decide which cards are required, which are completed, or what answering one changes. Those rules remain upstream. Clean operational boundary: display available assignments, request a random one, report when the queue is empty.
